6. Shade a rectangle to illustrate 3 1/3 ⋅ 1 2/3.
7. Leo says: "3 1/3 ⋅ 1 2/3 is simply 3 ⋅ 1 = 3, plus 1/3 ⋅ 2/3 = 2/9, a total of 3 2/9." Use your drawing from (6) to explain why Leo's work is wrong.
